_doc: PHI-54 — curated atlas of franchises and inspirations with real, locatable POIs. Edit Philip-curated PRs only. POIs must be verifiable; lat/lng plausible within the destination. Coverage: 2 seed franchises (HP, LOTR). Expand to the full 10 in follow-up PRs.

pois.name: Warner Bros Studio Tour London — The Making of Harry Potter
pois.blurb: Original sets, costumes, and props from all eight films at the Leavesden studios.

pois.name: Platform 9¾ — King's Cross Station
pois.blurb: The trolley-through-the-wall photo spot beside the King's Cross concourse.

pois.name: Leadenhall Market
pois.blurb: Filming location for Diagon Alley exterior shots in the Philosopher's Stone.

pois.name: House of MinaLima
pois.blurb: Gallery and shop run by the graphic designers behind the films' iconic prop art.

pois.name: The Elephant House (exterior)
pois.blurb: JK Rowling drafted early Potter chapters here; the cafe interior is currently closed but the exterior remains a pilgrimage point.

pois.name: Greyfriars Kirkyard
pois.blurb: Historic graveyard whose headstones include a Thomas Riddell Esquire — a likely source for Voldemort's name.

pois.name: Victoria Street
pois.blurb: Curved cobblestone street widely cited as Rowling's inspiration for Diagon Alley.

pois.name: Glenfinnan Viaduct
pois.blurb: The 21-arch viaduct featured in the Hogwarts Express scenes; Jacobite Steam Train crosses it daily in season.

pois.name: Hobbiton Movie Set
pois.blurb: The original Shire set kept intact for guided tours, including 44 hobbit holes and the Green Dragon Inn.

pois.name: Weta Workshop Tour
pois.blurb: Behind-the-scenes tour of the special-effects studio that built the films' creatures, weapons, and prosthetics.

pois.name: Mount Victoria Lookout
pois.blurb: Filming location for Hobbits-hiding-from-Black-Rider scene; panoramic city and harbour views.

pois.name: Glenorchy and the Dart River
pois.blurb: Lothlórien and Isengard backdrops; jet-boat or horse-trekking tours run from the lakefront.
